title: One-binary S5: binary-stable release channel (config-release vs binary-release classification) + docs/operations/corporate-deployment.md with the agy recipe
summary: OPERATOR-BOOSTED to second-block priority 2026-07-13. APPROVED PLAN slice 5/5 (dep S2). (1) Release pipeline classification step near the changelog step (releasepipeline.go:287): diff go/** since the last binary-release tag; unchanged => class=config-release, stamp 'binary fingerprint unchanged since vX.Y.Z' into CHANGELOG + release notes (no new approval needed); changed => class=binary-release (new fingerprint, approval required — policy: majors + security fixes). (2) New doc docs/operations/corporate-deployment.md: the agy corporate recipe — install the approved universal binary at a stable path via install.sh --binary; export EVOLVE_GO_BIN=<path> (FIRST in the skill's resolution order, platform-neutral: skills/loop/SKILL.md:21-27, same for agy projection); pin-at-install; approval-request flow using the Fingerprints manifest; the cert-based alternative (signing-identity/TeamID rules a la Santa CERTIFICATE => codesign+notarize once with a stable Developer ID = one approval EVER; operator decision, requires Apple Developer account); note on locally-compiled go test binaries being governed by company local-build policy, out of scope of distribution.
acceptance: release pipeline stamps the release class + fingerprint continuity into CHANGELOG and release notes (test with a docs-only tag simulation)
docs/operations/corporate-deployment.md exists, cross-linked from runtime-reference.md and README
